issues: Technical report mislabeled daily trend as bearish despite bullish structure — analysts caught it but the upstream data quality flag should be automated
1h volume anomaly (0.05 vol_ma_ratio) contradicts 24h volume ($62B) — timeframe mismatch between spot 1h snapshots and aggregate daily volume not reconciled
macro_tools module still missing from tradingagents — recurring issue across multiple runs degrading macro analyst capability
Fund manager reasoning was thin — essentially rubber-stamped the trader without independent analysis
improvements: Add automated data quality scoring that flags label-indicator contradictions before they reach analysts — the bearish label on a structurally bullish daily chart should never have shipped
Reconcile volume across timeframes explicitly: if 24h volume is elevated but 1h is 5% of MA, note this is a snapshot artifact vs structural weakness
Fix the missing tradingagents.tools / macro_tools module — this has been broken since Mar 3 and is degrading every run
Fund manager should add independent reasoning beyond process-checking the chain — even a HOLD decision deserves a paragraph of original thought
summary: System correctly held through an +8% BTC rally by identifying the critical volume non-confirmation (negative OBV, 0.05 vol_ma_ratio) beneath the price move. The bull/bear debate was genuinely substantive — the bear's volume objection was the strongest single argument and correctly won. The HOLD was the right call: buying after an 8% move with stochastics at 93-100 and no volume backing into a geopolitical weekend would have been reckless. Main drag is recurring macro_tools module failure and a mislabeled daily trend that required manual correction.
